title: 5 First-Time Puppy Owner Mistakes to Avoid
points: Not over-buying gear before day one
Not panicking at normal week-one behaviour
preview:
🐾 From two years of real placements: puppies thrive with a routine + someone to call.

The five mistakes we see most?
✓ Choosing by photo, not routine
✓ Over-buying gear before day one
✓ The 2am rescue that teaches the wrong lesson
✓ Chaotic socialisation instead of calm exposure
✓ Panicking at normal week-one behaviour

Every one is preventable. Read real placement stories and how to avoid them.

title: HDB Dogs in Singapore: A Complete Guide
points: HDB dog ownership is legal with correct breeds
Approved breeds are smaller, lower-shedding types
Noise management is key for neighbours
preview:
🏢 HDB Dogs in Singapore: What You Actually Need to Know

Thinking about getting a puppy in your HDB flat? Here's what's real:

✓ HDB dogs ARE legal (with the right breeds)
✓ Approved breeds list exists—Maltipoos & Cavapoos qualify
✓ Microchip + registration are non-negotiable
✓ Neighbours matter—training matters more
✓ Done right, flat life is perfect for the right puppy

Read what HDB actually allows, which breeds work best, and how to be the neighbour everyone likes.

📖 Full guide: https://curioustails.sg/blog/hdb-dog-guide

title: Cavapoo vs Maltipoo: Which Breed for You?
points: Cavapoos are more independent, better for 9-5 families
Maltipoos need more company and grooming
Both are great in HDB if trained
Routine and consistency are what make the difference
preview:
🐕 Cavapoo vs Maltipoo: Which Breed Is Right for Your Singapore Home?

Can't decide between a Cavapoo and Maltipoo? Let's cut through the photo-browsing and talk routine.

✓ Cavapoos = more independent, better for working parents
✓ Maltipoos = need more company, love being with you
✓ Both are HDB-approved (if trained)
✓ Both need regular grooming in Singapore's humidity
✓ The real difference? Your daily routine, not the breed look

Read what actually matters when choosing, what each breed needs, and which one matches your life.

📖 Compare breeds: https://curioustails.sg/blog/cavapoo-vs-maltipoo

title: Getting Your Puppy Through NDP and New Year Fireworks
points: Singapore's big fireworks are predictable: National Day and the New Year countdown
Prep starts on the NDP rehearsal nights, not just 9 August
Turn the starter-kit playpen into a covered safe room, aircon on to muffle the bangs
Desensitise 3 to 4 weeks early: low-volume sounds paired with treats
Comforting a scared puppy does not spoil it, and you should never punish the fear
preview:
🎆 Is your dog scared of fireworks? Here's how to get your puppy through National Day calm.

Singapore's big displays are predictable, so you can prepare properly:
✓ The fireworks start on the NDP rehearsal nights, not just 9 August
✓ Build a covered safe room from the starter-kit playpen, aircon on to soften the bangs
✓ Desensitise 3 to 4 weeks early: low volume, treats, calm
✓ Walk early, feed early, then just sit with them
✓ Never punish the fear, and comforting your puppy is completely fine

Do it early, and the ninth is just another night.

📖 Read the full plan: https://curioustails.sg/blog/puppy-fireworks-singapore

title: Surviving Your Puppy's First Night at Home
points: Most puppies cry for 20 to 40 minutes after lights-out, then settle
Set up the playpen next to your bed before you even leave to collect the puppy
A worn t-shirt with your scent works better than any comfort toy
Respond to sustained crying, since it's often a genuine toilet need, but don't rush in for every whimper
Keep 2am toilet trips boring: no play, no cuddles, straight back into the pen
preview:
🌙 What actually happens on your puppy's first night home?

Based on real delivery-day setups across Singapore:
✓ Most crying settles within 20 to 40 minutes if the pen is set up right
✓ Pen goes next to your bed, not in another room
✓ A worn t-shirt beats any $40 comfort toy
✓ Respond to sustained crying (likely a toilet need), not every whimper
✓ Keep 2am trips boring so it doesn't become a nightly habit

Get the full night-one routine:
https://curioustails.sg/blog/puppy-first-night
